definitions.definition: The layer of gases that surrounds the Earth, extending from the surface up to about 10,000 km.
The lowest layer of the atmosphere, extending up to about 12 km above the Earth's surface.
The second layer of the atmosphere, extending from the troposphere up to about 50 km above the Earth's surface.
The third layer of the atmosphere, extending from the stratosphere up to about 80 km above the Earth's surface.
The fourth layer of the atmosphere, extending from the mesosphere up to about 500 km above the Earth's surface.
The outermost layer of the atmosphere, extending from the thermosphere up to about 10,000 km above the Earth's surface.
A layer of ozone gas (O3) present in the stratosphere, protecting life on Earth from harmful UV radiation.
The process by which certain gases in the atmosphere trap heat from the sun, keeping the Earth warm and habitable.
The rate of change of temperature with respect to height in the atmosphere.
The rate at which the temperature of the atmosphere decreases with an increase in height.
The force exerted by the weight of the atmosphere on the Earth's surface.
The pressure exerted by the atmosphere at a particular location on the Earth's surface.
The amount of water vapor present in the atmosphere.
The process by which water from the Earth's surface is transformed into water vapor and rises into the atmosphere.
The process by which water vapor in the atmosphere changes back into liquid water, forming clouds, fog, or precipitation.
Any form of water, such as rain, snow, sleet, or hail, that falls to the Earth's surface from the atmosphere.
